name: Ralph Bunche
description: diplomat, educator, civil rights activist and the first African American Nobel Peace Prize winner (1904–1971)
abstract: Ralph Johnson Bunche ( BUNCH; August 7, 1904 – December 9, 1971) was an American political scientist and diplomat who received the 1950 Nobel Peace Prize for his late 1940s mediation in the Arab–Israeli conflict. He is the first black Nobel laureate and the first person of African descent to be awarded a Nobel Prize.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: That high school today is 100 percent black, Jefferson High School. It's the same school, as a matter of fact, that Ralph Bunch graduated from in the first graduation class, I think around 1916. I think he was in the first graduation class. Ralph Bunch was a pretty famous guy.
